channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Metformin Raises Exercise-Associated Metabolite Lac-Phe in Prostate Cancer Patients on Hormone Therapy
item.description: A new study from Sylvester Comprehensive Cancer Center at the University of Miami Miller School of Medicine found that the common diabetes drug metformin can increase levels of N-lactoyl-phenylalanine...

item.title: PM Shehbaz announces significant fuel price reduction ahead of US-Iran peace talks
item.description: ISLAMABAD: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has announced a significant reduction in petroleum prices across Pakistan, cutting petrol by Rs12 and diesel by Rs135 per litre. The move comes as the country ...

item.title: US Refugee Policy Changes Primarily Affect South African Applicants
item.description: Recent government data reveals that changes implemented by US President Donald Trump have drastically altered the refugee admissions process, both in volume and origin. Between October 2025 and March ...

item.title: Tamil Nadu dairy farmers seek proper support and fair pricing for milk production
item.description: Dairy farmers in Tamil Nadu have expressed deep concern over the lack of sustained attention to the fundamental issues affecting their livelihoods. They point out that the government has mainly priori...

item.title: Exploring Tweed Heads: National Parks, Volcanic Landscapes and Local Cuisine
item.description: Tweed Heads, nestled behind the Gold Coast in NSW, is a largely undiscovered gem combining lush national parks, dramatic volcanic landscapes, and a vibrant culinary scene. Often overshadowed by Byron ...

item.title: Thirteen Men Fined and Given Suspended Sentences Following Barbers' Street Brawl in South Wales
item.description: Thirteen men were involved in a violent street brawl in South Wales on 13 February, stemming from a dispute between two Turkish barber shops. Staff from Kurds Barbers in Newbridge travelled to Blackwo...

item.title: Former Shoprite Cash Office Employee Sentenced to Five Years for Theft
item.description: A former Shoprite employee, Dimakatso Tshipane, has been sentenced to five years' imprisonment for stealing R400,000 from her employer. The Bloemfontein regional court found Tshipane guilty of theft, ...

item.title: South Africa Strengthens Foot-and-Mouth Disease Vaccination Programme Amid Biosecurity Concerns
item.description: South Africa is ramping up its vaccination efforts to curb the ongoing foot-and-mouth disease (FMD) epidemic among cloven-hooved animals. The Department of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries, in coll...

item.title: Philippine Students Achieve 12 Medals at 2026 Stemco International Science Contest
item.description: The Philippine delegation excelled in the 2026 Stemco International Science competition held in Singapore, bringing home a total of 12 awards. The 12-member team, trained by the non-government Mathema...

item.title: Manila City Offers Financial Aid to Local Bakeries to Stabilize Bread Prices
item.description: MANILA, Philippines — The Manila city government, under Mayor Isko Moreno, has announced a P25,000 subsidy for community bakeries to maintain affordable bread prices for 30 days. The financial support...

item.title: Gmail Now Offers End-to-End Encryption on Mobile for Enterprise Users
item.description: Google has officially launched end-to-end encryption (E2EE) for Gmail on Android and iOS devices, bringing enhanced privacy and data security to users, particularly enterprise clients. This feature al...

item.title: Raphael Tuju Reflects on Surviving Plane and Road Accidents
item.description: Raphael Tuju, former Cabinet Secretary and Rarieda MP, recently returned to his home village in Siaya to share deeply personal reflections on life, survival, and threats he faces. Tuju recounted two m...

item.title: Study Identifies 102 Medicinal Plants for Managing Sickle Cell Disease in Tanzania
item.description: A recent study has documented 102 plant species used by traditional health practitioners (THPs) to manage sickle cell disease (SCD) in Tanzania's Lake Zone. Conducted across the Mwanza and Mara region...

item.title: Rigathi Gachagua Boasts of Confronting William Ruto at Nyandarua Funeral
item.description: In a rally in Machakos County, former Deputy President Rigathi Gachagua expressed his satisfaction after confronting President William Ruto during a requiem mass in Nyandarua. Gachagua proudly recalle...

item.title: Muslim Voters Shift from Labour to the Greens Amid Growing Concerns
item.description: In Newcastle, a significant shift is taking place among Muslim voters, with many turning away from the Labour Party in favour of the Greens. This change is partly driven by Labour’s perceived failure ...

item.title: US Inflation Rises in March Amid Ongoing Conflict with Iran
item.description: Inflation in the US surged in March, driven primarily by the war between the US and Israel on one side, and Iran on the other. Consumer prices jumped by 0.9% in March, and 3.3% over the year, marking ...

item.title: Justice Yashwant Varma Resigns and Withdraws from Parliamentary Cash Inquiry Citing Procedural Irregularities
item.description: Justice Yashwant Varma, an Allahabad High Court judge, has officially withdrawn from the parliamentary inquiry into the recovery of unaccounted cash at his official residence following a fire in March...

item.title: Common English Mistakes and Linguistic Insights from Tanzanian Media
item.description: In this tongue-in-cheek opinion piece, columnist Abdi Sultani comments on the misuse of English among Tanzanian journalists, especially those working in Kiswahili radio and TV stations. He laments tha...

item.title: Backdrop Collapses During Pennsylvania Governor's America250 Concert Announcement
item.description: During a Pennsylvania event announcing free concerts to celebrate America’s 250th birthday, a large 'America250PA' backdrop unexpectedly fell, nearly hitting Governor Josh Shapiro, a Benjamin Franklin...

item.title: Miss World Tanzania 2026 Final and Miss Grand Tanzania Auditions Scheduled for April in Dar es Salaam
item.description: Tanzania's beauty pageant season is heating up this April with two major events taking place in Dar es Salaam. The Miss World Tanzania 2026 Final is scheduled for April 19 at the Super Dome in Masaki,...
